availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.name: pr-comments
availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Review a pull request

availableCommands.name: security-review
availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

availableCommands.name: extra-usage
availableCommands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

availableCommands.name: insights
availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

```
     1→import { app } from 'electron'
     2→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, renameSync, statSync } from 'fs'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { syncMetaProjects } from './meta-project-sync'
     5→
     6→export interface ProjectCategory {
     7→  id: string
     8→  name: string
     9→  collapsed: boolean
    10→  order: number
    11→}
    12→
    13→export interface Project {
    14→  path: string
    15→  name: string
    16→  executable?: string
    17→  apiPort?: number  // Port for HTTP API to send prompts to terminal
    18→  apiAutoStart?: boolean  // Whether to auto-start API when session opens (default: false)
    19→  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'  // How API requests handle sessions
    20→  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'  // Model for API-triggered sessions
    21→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]  // Per-project tool patterns to auto-accept
    22→  permissionMode?: string     // Per-project permission mode
    23→  color?: string              // Project color for visual identification
    24→  ttsVoice?: string           // Per-project TTS voice (overrides global)
    25→  ttsEngine?: 'piper' | 'xtts'  // Per-project TTS engine
    26→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' // Per-project backend (overrides global)
    27→  categoryId?: string         // Category this project belongs to
    28→  order?: number              // Order within category or uncategorized list
    29→}
    30→
    31→export interface OpenTab {
    32→  id: string
    33→  projectPath: string
    34→  sessionId?: string
    35→  title: string
    36→  ptyId?: string
    37→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    38→}
    39→
    40→export interface TileLayout {
    41→  id: string
    42→  tabIds: string[]
    43→  activeTabId: string
    44→  x: number
    45→  y: number
    46→  width: number
    47→  height: number
    48→}
    49→
    50→export interface Workspace {
    51→  projects: Project[]
    52→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    53→  activeTabId: string | null
    54→  viewMode?: 'tabs' | 'tiled'
    55→  tileLayout?: TileLayout[]
    56→  categories?: ProjectCategory[]
    57→}
    58→
    59→export interface WindowBounds {
    60→  x: number
    61→  y: number
    62→  width: number
    63→  height: number
    64→}
    65→
    66→export interface ThemeCustomization {
    67→  accentColor: string | null
    68→  backgroundColor: string | null
    69→  textColor: string | null
    70→  terminalColors: {
    71→    black?: string
    72→    red?: string
    73→    green?: string
    74→    yellow?: string
    75→    blue?: string
    76→    magenta?: string
    77→    cyan?: string
    78→    white?: string
    79→  } | null
    80→}
    81→
    82→export interface Settings {
    83→  defaultProjectDir: string
    84→  theme: string
    85→  themeCustomization?: ThemeCustomization | null
    86→  voiceOutputEnabled?: boolean
    87→  voiceVolume?: number
    88→  voiceSpeed?: number
    89→  voiceSkipOnNew?: boolean
    90→  voiceSilenceThreshold?: number
    91→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
    92→  permissionMode?: string
    93→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    94→}
    95→
    96→
    97→interface StoredData {
    98→  workspace: Workspace
    99→  windowBounds?: WindowBounds
   100→  settings?: Settings
   101→}
   102→
   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204→    return this.data.workspace
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210→    this.data = this.load()
   211→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219→      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220→      return
   221→    }
   222→    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223→    this.save()
   224→    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228→    return this.data.windowBounds
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232→    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233→    this.save()
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  getSettings(): Settings {
   237→    return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  saveSettings(settings: Settings): void {
   241→    this.data.settings = settings
   242→    this.save()
   243→  }
   244→}
   245→

1→import { BrowserWindow, dialog, ipcMain } from 'electron'
     2→import { join } from 'path'
     3→import { readdirSync, statSync } from 'fs'
     4→import { SessionStore } from '../../session-store.js'
     5→import { getMetaProjectsPath } from '../../meta-project-sync.js'
     6→import { discoverSessions } from '../../session-discovery.js'
     7→
     8→export function registerWorkspaceHandlers(
     9→  sessionStore: SessionStore,
    10→  getMainWindow: () => BrowserWindow | null
    11→): void {
    12→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:get', () => sessionStore.getWorkspace())
    13→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:save', (_, workspace) => sessionStore.saveWorkspace(workspace))
    14→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getMetaProjectsPath', () => getMetaProjectsPath())
    15→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getCategoryMetaPath', (_, categoryName: string) => {
    16→    const basePath = getMetaProjectsPath()
    17→    // Sanitize category name for filesystem
    18→    const safeName = categoryName.replace(/[/\\:*?"<>|]/g, '_')
    19→    return join(basePath, safeName)
    20→  })
    21→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProject', async () => {
    22→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    23→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    24→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    25→      title: 'Select Project Folder'
    26→    })
    27→    return result.canceled ? null : result.filePaths[0] || null
    28→  })
    29→
    30→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProjectsFromParent', async () => {
    31→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    32→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    33→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    34→      title: 'Select Parent Folder (all subdirectories will be added as projects)'
    35→    })
    36→    if (result.canceled || !result.filePaths[0]) return null
    37→
    38→    const parentDir = result.filePaths[0]
    39→
    40→    try {
    41→      const entries = readdirSync(parentDir, { withFileTypes: true })
    42→      const subdirs = entries
    43→        .filter((entry) => entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith('.'))
    44→        .map((entry) => ({
    45→          path: join(parentDir, entry.name),
    46→          name: entry.name
    47→        }))
    48→      return subdirs
    49→    } catch (e) {
    50→      console.error('Failed to scan parent directory:', e)
    51→      return null
    52→    }
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  // Session discovery
    56→  ipcMain.handle('sessions:discover', (_, projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => discoverSessions(projectPath, backend))
    57→}
    58→

     2→ * Settings Routes
     3→ *
     4→ * HTTP endpoints for application settings, voice configuration, and CLI status.

     6→
     7→import { Router, Request, Response } from 'express'
     8→import {
     9→  ApiResponse,
    10→  Settings,
    11→  VoiceSettings
    12→} from '../types'
    13→import { getServices } from '../app'
    14→
    15→const router = Router()
    16→
    17→// =============================================================================
    18→// Helper Functions
    19→// =============================================================================
    20→
    21→function sendResponse<T>(res: Response, statusCode: number, data: ApiResponse<T>): void {

   224→/**
   225→ * GET /api/settings/cli/status
   226→ * Get installation status of all CLI tools
   227→ *
   228→ * Returns: { claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider }
   229→ */
   230→router.get('/cli/status', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   231→  try {
   232→    const services = getServices()
   233→
   234→    const status: Record<string, any> = {}
   235→
   236→    // Check each CLI tool in parallel
   237→    const checks = await Promise.allSettled([
   238→      services.claudeCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   239→      services.geminiCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   240→      services.codexCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   241→      services.opencodeCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   242→      services.aiderCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false })
   243→    ])
   244→
   245→    const cliNames = ['claude', 'gemini', 'codex', 'opencode', 'aider']
   246→
   247→    checks.forEach((result, index) => {
   248→      if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
   249→        status[cliNames[index]] = result.value
   250→      } else {
   251→        status[cliNames[index]] = { installed: false, error: result.reason?.message }
   252→      }
   253→    })
   254→
   255→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
   256→      success: true,
   257→      data: status,
   258→      timestamp: Date.now()
   259→    })
   260→  } catch (error: any) {
   261→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to check CLI status')
   262→  }
   263→})
   264→
   265→/**
   266→ * GET /api/settings/cli/:tool
   267→ * Get installation status of a specific CLI tool
   268→ *
   269→ * Params: tool - one of: claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider
   270→ */
   271→router.get('/cli/:tool', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   272→  try {
   273→    const services = getServices()
   274→    const { tool } = req.params
   275→
   276→    const checkFunctions: Record<string, (() => Promise<any>) | undefined> = {
   277→      claude: services.claudeCheck,
   278→      gemini: services.geminiCheck,
   279→      codex: services.codexCheck,
   280→      opencode: services.opencodeCheck,
   281→      aider: services.aiderCheck
   282→    }
   283→
   284→    const checkFn = checkFunctions[tool]
   285→    if (!checkFn) {
   286→      return sendError(res, 400, `Unknown tool: ${tool}. Valid tools: ${Object.keys(checkFunctions).join(', ')}`)
   287→    }
   288→
   289→    const result = await checkFn()
   290→
   291→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
   292→      success: true,
   293→      data: result,
   294→      timestamp: Date.now()
   295→    })
   296→  } catch (error: any) {
   297→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to check CLI status')
   298→  }
   299→})
   300→
   301→export default router

    32→      data: workspace,
    33→      timestamp: Date.now()
    34→    })
    35→  } catch (error: any) {
    36→    console.error('[Projects Route] Get workspace error:', error)
    37→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to get workspace')
    38→  }
    39→})
    40→
    41→/**
    42→ * PUT /api/projects
    43→ * Save the full workspace
    44→ *
    45→ * Body: Workspace
    46→ */
    47→router.put('/', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    48→  try {
    49→    const services = getServices()
    50→
    51→    if (!services.saveWorkspace) {
    52→      return sendError(res, 503, 'Workspace service not available')
    53→    }
    54→
    55→    const workspace: Workspace = req.body
    56→
    57→    if (!workspace || !Array.isArray(workspace.projects)) {
    58→      return sendError(res, 400, 'Invalid workspace data')
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    await services.saveWorkspace(workspace)
    62→
    63→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
    64→      success: true,
    65→      timestamp: Date.now()
    66→    })
    67→  } catch (error: any) {
    68→    console.error('[Projects Route] Save workspace error:', error)
    69→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to save workspace')
    70→  }
    71→})
    72→
    73→/**
    74→ * GET /api/projects/list
    75→ * Get just the list of projects (convenience endpoint)
    76→ *
    77→ * Returns: Project[]
    78→ */
    79→router.get('/list', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
    80→  try {
    81→    const services = getServices()
    82→
    83→    if (!services.getWorkspace) {
    84→      return sendError(res, 503, 'Workspace service not available')
    85→    }
    86→
    87→    const workspace: Workspace = await services.getWorkspace()
    88→
    89→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
    90→      success: true,
    91→      data: workspace.projects,
    92→      timestamp: Date.now()
    93→    })
    94→  } catch (error: any) {
    95→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to get projects')
    96→  }
    97→})
    98→
    99→export default router
   100→

1→import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { Theme, getThemeById, applyTheme, themes } from '../themes'
     3→
     4→export interface TerminalColorsCustomization {
     5→  black?: string
     6→  red?: string
     7→  green?: string
     8→  yellow?: string
     9→  blue?: string
    10→  magenta?: string
    11→  cyan?: string
    12→  white?: string
    13→}
    14→
    15→export interface ThemeCustomization {
    16→  accentColor: string | null
    17→  backgroundColor: string | null
    18→  textColor: string | null
    19→  terminalColors: TerminalColorsCustomization | null
    20→}
    21→
    22→export interface AppSettings {
    23→  defaultProjectDir: string
    24→  theme: string
    25→  themeCustomization?: ThemeCustomization | null
    26→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
    27→  permissionMode?: string
    28→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    29→}
    30→
    31→interface UseSettingsReturn {
    32→  settings: AppSettings | null
    33→  currentTheme: Theme
    34→  loading: boolean
    35→  updateSettings: (newSettings: AppSettings) => void
    36→  updateTheme: (theme: Theme) => void
    37→}
    38→
    39→export function useSettings(): UseSettingsReturn {
    40→  const [settings, setSettings] = useState<AppSettings | null>(null)
    41→  const [currentTheme, setCurrentTheme] = useState<Theme>(themes[0])
    42→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    43→
    44→  // Load settings on mount
    45→  useEffect(() => {
    46→    if (!window.electronAPI) {
    47→      setLoading(false)
    48→      return
    49→    }
    50→    const loadSettings = async () => {
    51→      try {
    52→        const loadedSettings = await window.electronAPI?.getSettings()
    53→        if (!loadedSettings) {
    54→          return
    55→        }
    56→        setSettings(loadedSettings)
    57→
    58→        // Apply theme with customization
    59→        const theme = getThemeById(loadedSettings.theme || 'default')
    60→        applyTheme(theme, loadedSettings.themeCustomization)
    61→        setCurrentTheme(theme)
    62→      } catch (e) {
    63→        console.error('Failed to load settings:', e)
    64→      } finally {
    65→        setLoading(false)
    66→      }
    67→    }
    68→    loadSettings()
    69→  }, [])
    70→
    71→  const updateSettings = useCallback((newSettings: AppSettings) => {
    72→    setSettings(newSettings)
    73→  }, [])
    74→
    75→  const updateTheme = useCallback((theme: Theme) => {
    76→    applyTheme(theme, settings?.themeCustomization)
    77→    setCurrentTheme(theme)
    78→  }, [settings?.themeCustomization])
    79→
    80→  return {
    81→    settings,
    82→    currentTheme,
    83→    loading,
    84→    updateSettings,
    85→    updateTheme
    86→  }
    87→}
    88→

1→import { useEffect, useRef, useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { useWorkspaceStore } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→import { Theme, getThemeById, applyTheme, themes } from '../themes'
     7→import { cleanupOrphanedBuffers } from '../components/terminal/Terminal'
     8→
     9→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderOptions {
    10→  api: Api
    11→  checkInstallation: () => Promise<void>
    12→  setViewMode: (mode: 'tabs' | 'tiled') => void
    13→  setTileLayout: (layout: any[]) => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderReturn {
    17→  loading: boolean
    18→  currentTheme: Theme
    19→  settings: AppSettings | null
    20→  setCurrentTheme: (theme: Theme) => void
    21→  setSettings: (settings: AppSettings | null) => void
    22→}
    23→
    24→export function useWorkspaceLoader({
    25→  api,
    26→  checkInstallation,
    27→  setViewMode,
    28→  setTileLayout
    29→}: UseWorkspaceLoaderOptions): UseWorkspaceLoaderReturn {
    30→  const {
    31→    setProjects,
    32→    setCategories,
    33→    addTab,
    34→    clearTabs
    35→  } = useWorkspaceStore()
    36→
    37→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    38→  const [currentTheme, setCurrentTheme] = useState<Theme>(themes[0])
    39→  const [settings, setSettings] = useState<AppSettings | null>(null)
    40→  const initRef = useRef(false)
    41→  const hadProjectsRef = useRef(false)
    42→
    43→  // Load workspace on mount and restore tabs
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    // Prevent double initialization from StrictMode
    46→    if (initRef.current) return
    47→    initRef.current = true
    48→
    49→    const loadWorkspace = async () => {
    50→      try {
    51→        // Check if Claude, npm, and git-bash are installed
    52→        await checkInstallation()
    53→
    54→        // Load and apply theme with any saved customizations
    55→        const loadedSettings = await api.getSettings()
    56→        setSettings(loadedSettings)
    57→        const theme = getThemeById(loadedSettings.theme || 'default')
    58→        applyTheme(theme, loadedSettings.themeCustomization)
    59→        setCurrentTheme(theme)
    60→
    61→        // Kill any existing PTYs from hot reload (but don't clear buffers - they'll be restored)
    62→        const existingTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    63→        for (const tab of existingTabs) {
    64→          api.killPty(tab.id)
    65→        }
    66→        clearTabs()
    67→
    68→        const workspace = await api.getWorkspace()
    69→        if (workspace.projects) {
    70→          setProjects(workspace.projects)
    71→          if (workspace.projects.length > 0) {
    72→            hadProjectsRef.current = true
    73→          }
    74→          // Install TTS instructions for all existing projects
    75→          for (const project of workspace.projects) {
    76→            await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(project.path)
    77→          }
    78→        }
    79→        // Load categories
    80→        if (workspace.categories) {
    81→          setCategories(workspace.categories)
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        // Restore previously open tabs (only if not already open)
    85→        // Track old ID -> new ID mapping for layout restoration
    86→        const idMapping = new Map<string, string>()
    87→
    88→        if (workspace.openTabs && workspace.openTabs.length > 0) {
    89→          const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    90→          const usedSessionIds = new Set(currentTabs.map(t => t.sessionId).filter(Boolean))
    91→          const sessionsCache = new Map<string, { list: { sessionId: string; slug: string }[]; nextIndex: number }>()
    92→
    93→          for (const savedTab of workspace.openTabs) {
    94→            try {
    95→              // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    96→              await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(savedTab.projectPath)
    97→
    98→              const projectName = savedTab.projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || savedTab.projectPath
    99→              let titleToRestore = savedTab.title || `${projectName} - New`
   100→
   101→              // Get project and determine effective backend for restored tab
   102→              const projectForTab = workspace.projects?.find((p: { path: string }) => p.path === savedTab.projectPath)
   103→              const savedBackend = savedTab.backend
   104→              const effectiveBackendForTab = (savedBackend
   105→                || (projectForTab?.backend && projectForTab.backend !== 'default'
   106→                  ? projectForTab.backend
   107→                  : (loadedSettings?.backend && loadedSettings.backend !== 'default'
   108→                    ? loadedSettings.backend
   109→                    : 'claude'))) as BackendId
   110→
   111→              let sessionIdToRestore: string | undefined = savedTab.sessionId

   113→              // Always discover sessions to find the most recent one
   114→              let sessionsForProject = sessionsCache.get(savedTab.projectPath)
   115→              if (!sessionsForProject) {
   116→                const list = await api.discoverSessions(savedTab.projectPath, effectiveBackendForTab)
   117→                sessionsForProject = { list, nextIndex: 0 }
   118→                sessionsCache.set(savedTab.projectPath, sessionsForProject)
   119→              }
   120→
   121→              const list = sessionsForProject.list || []
   122→              for (let i = sessionsForProject.nextIndex; i < list.length; i++) {
   123→                const candidate = list[i]
   124→                if (!usedSessionIds.has(candidate.sessionId)) {
   125→                  sessionIdToRestore = candidate.sessionId
   126→                  titleToRestore = `${projectName} - ${candidate.slug}`
   127→                  sessionsForProject.nextIndex = i + 1
   128→                  break
   129→                }
   130→              }

   132→              const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(

   139→                idMapping.set(savedTab.id, ptyId)
   140→              }

   145→                sessionId: sessionIdToRestore,
   146→                title: titleToRestore,
   147→                ptyId,

   149→              })

   152→              }
   153→            } catch (e) {

   155→            }

   160→        const activeTabIds = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.map(t => t.id)
   161→        cleanupOrphanedBuffers(activeTabIds)

   163→        // Restore view mode

   168→        if (workspace.tileLayout && workspace.tileLayout.length > 0 && idMapping.size > 0) {
   169→          const mappedLayout = workspace.tileLayout
   170→            .map(tile => {

   181→              if (mappedTabIds.length === 0) return null
   182→
   183→              const newId = idMapping.get(tile.id) || mappedTabIds[0]

   185→                ? (idMapping.get(tile.activeTabId) || mappedTabIds[0])
   186→                : mappedTabIds[0]

   188→              return {

   190→                id: newId,
   191→                tabIds: mappedTabIds,

   193→              }
   194→            })
   195→            .filter((tile): tile is NonNullable<typeof tile> => tile !== null)
   196→
   197→          if (mappedLayout.length > 0) {

   199→          }

     8→
     9→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderOptions {
    10→  api: Api
    11→  checkInstallation: () => Promise<void>
    12→  setViewMode: (mode: 'tabs' | 'tiled') => void
    13→  setTileLayout: (layout: any[]) => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→interface UseWorkspaceLoaderReturn {
    17→  loading: boolean
    18→  currentTheme: Theme
    19→  settings: AppSettings | null

    23→
    24→export function useWorkspaceLoader({
    25→  api,
    26→  checkInstallation,

    40→  const initRef = useRef(false)

    42→
    43→  // Load workspace on mount and restore tabs
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    // Prevent double initialization from StrictMode
    46→    if (initRef.current) return
    47→    initRef.current = true
    48→
    49→    const loadWorkspace = async () => {
    50→      try {
    51→        // Check if Claude, npm, and git-bash are installed
    52→        await checkInstallation()
    53→
    54→        // Load and apply theme with any saved customizations
    55→        const loadedSettings = await api.getSettings()
    56→        setSettings(loadedSettings)
    57→        const theme = getThemeById(loadedSettings.theme || 'default')
    58→        applyTheme(theme, loadedSettings.themeCustomization)
    59→        setCurrentTheme(theme)
    60→
    61→        // Kill any existing PTYs from hot reload (but don't clear buffers - they'll be restored)
    62→        const existingTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    63→        for (const tab of existingTabs) {
    64→          api.killPty(tab.id)
    65→        }
    66→        clearTabs()
    67→
    68→        const workspace = await api.getWorkspace()
    69→        if (workspace.projects) {
    70→          setProjects(workspace.projects)
    71→          if (workspace.projects.length > 0) {
    72→            hadProjectsRef.current = true
    73→          }

    75→          for (const project of workspace.projects) {
    76→            await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(project.path)
    77→          }
    78→        }
    79→        // Load categories
    80→        if (workspace.categories) {
    81→          setCategories(workspace.categories)
    82→        }
    83→
    84→        // Restore previously open tabs (only if not already open)
    85→        // Track old ID -> new ID mapping for layout restoration
    86→        const idMapping = new Map<string, string>()
    87→
    88→        if (workspace.openTabs && workspace.openTabs.length > 0) {
    89→          const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
    90→          const usedSessionIds = new Set(currentTabs.map(t => t.sessionId).filter(Boolean))
    91→          const sessionsCache = new Map<string, { list: { sessionId: string; slug: string }[]; nextIndex: number }>()
    92→
    93→          for (const savedTab of workspace.openTabs) {
    94→            try {
    95→              // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    96→              await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(savedTab.projectPath)
    97→
    98→              const projectName = savedTab.projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || savedTab.projectPath
    99→              let titleToRestore = savedTab.title || `${projectName} - New`
   100→
   101→              // Get project and determine effective backend for restored tab

   103→              const savedBackend = savedTab.backend

   105→                || (projectForTab?.backend && projectForTab.backend !== 'default'
   106→                  ? projectForTab.backend
   107→                  : (loadedSettings?.backend && loadedSettings.backend !== 'default'
   108→                    ? loadedSettings.backend
   109→                    : 'claude'))) as BackendId
   110→

   127→                  sessionsForProject.nextIndex = i + 1
   128→                  break

   130→              }
   131→
   132→              const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(
   133→                savedTab.projectPath,
   134→                sessionIdToRestore,
   135→                undefined,
   136→                effectiveBackendForTab
   137→              )
   138→              if (savedTab.id) {
   139→                idMapping.set(savedTab.id, ptyId)
   140→              }
   141→
   142→              addTab({
   143→                id: ptyId,
   144→                projectPath: savedTab.projectPath,
   145→                sessionId: sessionIdToRestore,
   146→                title: titleToRestore,
   147→                ptyId,

   149→              })
   150→              if (sessionIdToRestore) {
   151→                usedSessionIds.add(sessionIdToRestore)

   153→            } catch (e) {
   154→              console.error('Failed to restore tab:', savedTab.projectPath, e)
   155→            }
   156→          }
   157→        }
   158→
   159→        // Clean up orphaned terminal buffers from previous session/HMR
   160→        const activeTabIds = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.map(t => t.id)
   161→        cleanupOrphanedBuffers(activeTabIds)
   162→
   163→        // Restore view mode
   164→        if (workspace.viewMode) {
   165→          setViewMode(workspace.viewMode)
   166→        }
   167→        // Restore tileLayout with mapped IDs and migrate legacy format
   168→        if (workspace.tileLayout && workspace.tileLayout.length > 0 && idMapping.size > 0) {
   169→          const mappedLayout = workspace.tileLayout
   170→            .map(tile => {
   171→              // Migrate legacy tiles: add tabIds/activeTabId if missing
   172→              const tabIds = tile.tabIds || [tile.id]
   173→              const mappedTabIds = tabIds
   174→                .map(id => idMapping.get(id) || id)

   177→                  const currentTabs = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs
   178→                  return currentTabs.some(t => t.id === id)

   180→
   181→              if (mappedTabIds.length === 0) return null
   182→
   183→              const newId = idMapping.get(tile.id) || mappedTabIds[0]
   184→              const activeTabId = tile.activeTabId
   185→                ? (idMapping.get(tile.activeTabId) || mappedTabIds[0])
   186→                : mappedTabIds[0]
   187→
   188→              return {
   189→                ...tile,
   190→                id: newId,

   197→          if (mappedLayout.length > 0) {

1→/**
     2→ * Electron Backend Implementation
     3→ *
     4→ * This module provides the Electron IPC implementation of the Api interface.
     5→ * It delegates all calls to window.electronAPI which communicates with the
     6→ * Electron main process via IPC.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import {
    10→  Api,
    11→  ExtendedApi,
    12→  Settings,
    13→  Workspace,
    14→  Session,
    15→  PtyDataCallback,
    16→  PtyExitCallback,
    17→  PtyRecreatedCallback,
    18→  ApiOpenSessionCallback,
    19→  Unsubscribe
    20→} from './types'
    21→import type { BackendId } from './types'
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Type declaration for the global electronAPI
    25→ */
    26→declare global {
    27→  interface Window {
    28→    electronAPI?: {
    29→      // PTY Management
    30→      spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<string>
    31→      killPty: (id: string) => void

    37→      setPtyBackend: (id: string, backend: BackendId) => Promise<void>
    38→
    39→
    40→      // Session Management
    41→      discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<Session[]>
    42→
    43→      // Workspace Management
    44→      getWorkspace: () => Promise<Workspace>
    45→      saveWorkspace: (workspace: Workspace) => Promise<void>
    46→
    47→      // Settings Management
    48→      getSettings: () => Promise<Settings>
    49→      saveSettings: (settings: Settings) => Promise<void>
    50→
    51→      // Project Management
    52→      addProject: () => Promise<string | null>

    54→
    55→      // TTS
    56→      ttsInstallInstructions: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>

    58→      xttsGetVoices?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; language: string; createdAt: number }>>
    59→      voiceGetSettings?: () => Promise<{ ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }>
    60→      voiceCheckWhisper?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; models: string[]; currentModel: string | null }>
    61→      voiceCheckTTS?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; engine: string | null; voices: string[]; currentVoice: string | null }>
    62→      voiceInstallWhisper?: (model: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
    63→      voiceApplySettings?: (settings: { ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    64→      voiceSetVoice?: (voice: string | { voice: string; engine: 'piper' | 'xtts' }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    65→      ttsRemoveInstructions?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    66→      extensionsGetInstalled?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; type: string }>>
    67→      voiceSpeak: (text: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }>
    68→      voiceStopSpeaking: () => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    69→
    70→      // Events
    71→      onApiOpenSession: (callback: (data: { projectPath: string; autoClose: boolean; model?: string }) => void) => () => void
    72→
    73→      // API Server

    76→
    77→      // Extended API (Desktop-only)
    78→      selectDirectory: () => Promise<string | null>

    80→      windowMinimize: () => void

    84→      getPathForFile: (file: File) => string

    86→      getVersion: () => Promise<string>
    87→      isDebugMode: () => Promise<boolean>
    88→      refresh: () => Promise<void>
    89→      openExternal: (url: string) => Promise<void>
    90→      debugLog: (message: string) => void
    91→
    92→      // Beads
    93→      beadsCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ installed: boolean; initialized: boolean }>

    95→      beadsInstall: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; method?: string; needsPython?: boolean }>
    96→      beadsReady: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; tasks?: unknown[]; error?: string }>
    97→      beadsList: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; tasks?: unknown[]; error?: string }>
    98→      beadsShow: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; task?: unknown; error?: string }>
    99→      beadsCreate: (cwd: string, title: string, description?: string, priority?: number, type?: string, labels?: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; task?: unknown; error?: string }>
   100→      beadsComplete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; result?: unknown; error?: string }>
   101→      beadsDelete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   102→      beadsStart: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>

   106→      onBeadsTasksChanged: (callback: (data: { cwd: string }) => void) => () => void
   107→
   108→      // Kspec
   109→      kspecCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ exists: boolean }>
   110→      kspecInit: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   111→      kspecEnsureDaemon: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; alreadyRunning?: boolean; error?: string }>
   112→      kspecCheckCli: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; version?: string }>
   113→      kspecInstallCli: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   114→      kspecMigrateFromBeads: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; migrated: number; error?: string }>
   115→      kspecDispatchStart: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   116→      kspecDispatchStop: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   117→      kspecDispatchStatus: (cwd: string) => Promise<{ running: boolean; [key: string]: unknown }>
   118→
   119→      // Install progress
   120→      pythonInstall: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; method?: string }>
   121→      onInstallProgress: (callback: (data: { type: string; status: string; percent?: number }) => void) => () => void
   122→    }
   123→  }
   124→}
   125→
   126→/**
   127→ * Electron backend implementation that delegates to window.electronAPI
   128→ */
   129→export class ElectronBackend implements ExtendedApi {
   130→  /**
   131→   * Check if the Electron API is available
   132→   * @throws Error if electronAPI is not available
   133→   */
   134→  private checkApi(): void {
   135→    if (!window.electronAPI) {
   136→      throw new Error('Electron API not available. Are you running in Electron?')
   137→    }
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  // ==========================================================================
   141→  // PTY Management
   142→  // ==========================================================================
   143→
   144→  async spawnPty(cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<string> {
   145→    this.checkApi()
   146→    return window.electronAPI!.spawnPty(cwd, sessionId, model, backend)
   147→  }
   148→
   149→  killPty(id: string): void {
   150→    this.checkApi()
   151→    window.electronAPI!.killPty(id)
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  writePty(id: string, data: string): void {
   155→    this.checkApi()
   156→    window.electronAPI!.writePty(id, data)
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  resizePty(id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void {
   160→    this.checkApi()
   161→    window.electronAPI!.resizePty(id, cols, rows)
   162→  }
   163→
   164→  onPtyData(id: string, callback: PtyDataCallback): Unsubscribe {
   165→    this.checkApi()
   166→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyData(id, callback)
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  onPtyExit(id: string, callback: PtyExitCallback): Unsubscribe {
   170→    this.checkApi()
   171→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyExit(id, callback)
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  onPtyRecreated(callback: PtyRecreatedCallback): Unsubscribe {
   175→    this.checkApi()
   176→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyRecreated(callback)
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  setPtyBackend(id: string, backend: BackendId): Promise<void> {
   180→    this.checkApi()
   181→    return window.electronAPI!.setPtyBackend(id, backend)
   182→  }
   183→
   184→  // ==========================================================================
   185→  // Session Management
   186→  // ==========================================================================
   187→
   188→  async discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<Session[]> {
   189→    this.checkApi()
   190→    return window.electronAPI!.discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)
   191→  }
   192→
   193→  // ==========================================================================
   194→  // Workspace Management
   195→  // ==========================================================================
   196→
   197→  async getWorkspace(): Promise<Workspace> {
   198→    this.checkApi()
   199→    return window.electronAPI!.getWorkspace()
   200→  }
   201→
   202→  async saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): Promise<void> {
   203→    this.checkApi()
   204→    return window.electronAPI!.saveWorkspace(workspace)
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // ==========================================================================
   208→  // Settings Management
   209→  // ==========================================================================
   210→
   211→  async getSettings(): Promise<Settings> {
   212→    this.checkApi()
   213→    return window.electronAPI!.getSettings()
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  async saveSettings(settings: Settings): Promise<void> {
   217→    this.checkApi()
   218→    return window.electronAPI!.saveSettings(settings)
   219→  }
   220→

1→/**
     2→ * HTTP/WebSocket Backend Implementation
     3→ *
     4→ * This module implements the Api interface using HTTP requests and WebSocket
     5→ * connections to communicate with the mobile server running on a desktop host.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import {
     9→  Api,
    10→  ConnectionState,
    11→  Settings,
    12→  Workspace,
    13→  Session,
    14→  PtyDataCallback,
    15→  PtyExitCallback,
    16→  PtyRecreatedCallback,
    17→  ApiOpenSessionCallback,
    18→  Unsubscribe,
    19→  BackendId
    20→} from '../types'
    21→import { HttpBackendConfig } from './types'
    22→import { DEFAULT_PORT } from './constants'
    23→import { isLocalNetwork } from './helpers'
    24→import { ConnectionManager } from './connection'
    25→import { PtyWebSocketManager } from './pty-websocket'
    26→import { PtyApi } from './pty-api'
    27→import { WorkspaceApi } from './workspace-api'
    28→
    29→export class HttpBackend implements Api {
    30→  voiceGetInstalled?: () => Promise<
    31→    Array<{
    32→      key: string
    33→      displayName: string
    34→      source: 'builtin' | 'downloaded' | 'custom'
    35→      quality?: string
    36→      language?: string
    37→    }>
    38→  >
    39→  xttsGetVoices?: () => Promise<
    40→    Array<{ id: string; name: string; language: string; createdAt: number }>
    41→  >
    42→  voiceGetSettings?: () => Promise<{ ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string }>
    43→  apiStart?: (
    44→    projectPath: string,
    45→    port: number
    46→  ) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
    47→  apiStop?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    48→
    49→  private host: string
    50→  private port: number
    51→  private connection: ConnectionManager
    52→  private wsManager: PtyWebSocketManager
    53→  private ptyApi: PtyApi
    54→  private workspaceApi: WorkspaceApi
    55→
    56→  constructor(config: HttpBackendConfig) {
    57→    // Validate port to prevent requests to invalid addresses like localhost:1
    58→    if (
    59→      !config.port ||
    60→      config.port < 1 ||
    61→      config.port > 65535 ||
    62→      !Number.isInteger(config.port)
    63→    ) {
    64→      console.error('[HttpBackend] Invalid port:', config.port, '- using default', DEFAULT_PORT)
    65→      config.port = DEFAULT_PORT
    66→    }
    67→    if (config.port < 1024) {
    68→      console.warn(
    69→        '[HttpBackend] Port',
    70→        config.port,
    71→        'is a privileged port (< 1024), this may fail'
    72→      )
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    this.host = config.host
    76→    this.port = config.port
    77→
    78→    // Determine protocol based on whether host is local
    79→    const httpProtocol = isLocalNetwork(config.host) ? 'http' : 'https'
    80→    const wsProtocol = isLocalNetwork(config.host) ? 'ws' : 'wss'
    81→
    82→    const baseUrl = `${httpProtocol}://${config.host}:${config.port}`
    83→    const wsBaseUrl = `${wsProtocol}://${config.host}:${config.port}`
    84→
    85→    console.log('[HttpBackend] Initialized with baseUrl:', baseUrl)
    86→
    87→    // Initialize managers
    88→    this.connection = new ConnectionManager(baseUrl, config.token)
    89→    this.wsManager = new PtyWebSocketManager(wsBaseUrl, config.token)
    90→    this.ptyApi = new PtyApi(this.connection, this.wsManager)
    91→    this.workspaceApi = new WorkspaceApi(this.connection)
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  // Connection State Management
    95→
    96→  getConnectionState(): ConnectionState {
    97→    return this.connection.getConnectionState()
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  getConnectionError(): string | null {
   101→    return this.connection.getConnectionError()
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  onConnectionStateChange(callback: (state: ConnectionState) => void): Unsubscribe {
   105→    return this.connection.onConnectionStateChange(callback)
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  // PTY Management
   109→
   110→  spawnPty(cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<string> {
   111→    return this.ptyApi.spawnPty(cwd, sessionId, model, backend)
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  killPty(id: string): void {
   115→    this.ptyApi.killPty(id)
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  writePty(id: string, data: string): void {
   119→    this.ptyApi.writePty(id, data)
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  resizePty(id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void {
   123→    this.ptyApi.resizePty(id, cols, rows)
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  onPtyData(id: string, callback: PtyDataCallback): Unsubscribe {
   127→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyData(id, callback)
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  onPtyExit(id: string, callback: PtyExitCallback): Unsubscribe {
   131→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyExit(id, callback)
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  onPtyRecreated(callback: PtyRecreatedCallback): Unsubscribe {
   135→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyRecreated(callback)
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  // Session Management
   139→
   140→  discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<Session[]> {
   141→    return this.workspaceApi.discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  // Workspace Management
   145→
   146→  getWorkspace(): Promise<Workspace> {
   147→    return this.workspaceApi.getWorkspace()
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): Promise<void> {

file.content:
import { ipcRenderer } from 'electron'
import type { Workspace } from '../types/workspace.js'

export const workspaceHandlers = {
  getWorkspace: (): Promise<Workspace> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:get'),
  saveWorkspace: (workspace: Workspace): Promise<void> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:save', workspace),
  addProject: (): Promise<string | null> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:addProject'),
  addProjectsFromParent: (): Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:addProjectsFromParent'),
  getMetaProjectsPath: (): Promise<string> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:getMetaProjectsPath'),
  getCategoryMetaPath: (categoryName: string): Promise<string> => ipcRenderer.invoke('workspace:getCategoryMetaPath', categoryName),

  discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') =>
    ipcRenderer.invoke('sessions:discover', projectPath, backend)
}

Thoroughly explore the workspace persistence and settings code in this Electron app. I need to audit for bugs. Focus on:

1. src/main/session-store.ts - the main persistence layer
2. src/main/meta-project-sync.ts - symlink management  
3. src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts - workspace HTTP routes
4. Any settings-related files (search for settings save/load patterns)
5. src/renderer/ components that consume workspace/settings data

Look for:
- Race conditions in file I/O
- Data loss scenarios
- Incorrect error handling
- State synchronization bugs
- Settings not persisting correctly
- Any workspace-related bugs

Read all relevant files completely and report findings with file:line references.

Thoroughness: very thorough

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive audit. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive Audit Report: Workspace Persistence and Settings Code

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've identified several bugs and potential issues across the persistence layer. Here are my findings organized by severity:

### CRITICAL BUGS

**1. POST /api/workspace Endpoint: Incorrect Property Path (Line 75)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts`

The POST endpoint checks for `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` (line 75) while the PUT endpoint checks `incoming?.projects?.length` (line 51). This is inconsistent and will cause validation failures.

```
Line 75: const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
Line 78: const currentProjects = current?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
```

The SessionStore's `saveWorkspace()` method (session-store.ts:222) receives a `Workspace` object directly, not wrapped in a `{ workspace: ... }` structure. The POST endpoint is checking the wrong property path, causing its data validation to always fail silently.

**Expected behavior**: Both endpoints should check `incoming?.projects?.length`

---

**2. Race Condition in saveWorkspace() When Backup Update Fails (Lines 178-182)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

The backup is created AFTER validating the incoming workspace but BEFORE the atomic write completes. If the application crashes between creating the backup and completing the rename, the backup may be stale and recovery will restore outdated data.

```typescript
// Line 174-187: Backup logic
if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
  try {
    const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
    if (stat.size > 0) {
      const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
      if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
        writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
      }
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)  // Silent failure
  }
}
// Line 189-197: Atomic write happens here
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
```

**Issue**: If backup update fails with a logged error, the save continues anyway to a temp file. But if the rename fails after that, the system falls back to the old corrupted main file (not the new backup).

---

**3. Settings Not Persisting in SettingsModal.tsx (Line 178)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx`

The `handleSave()` function saves application settings and voice settings separately, but the `onSaved` callback (line 178) passes only the `newSettings` object, omitting voice settings. The voice settings are saved via a separate call to `voiceApplySettings` (lines 169-177), but if that promise rejects, it's not awaited.

```typescript
async function handleSave(): Promise<void> {
  const newSettings = { ... }
  await window.electronAPI?.saveSettings(newSettings)
  // Line 169-177: This promise is NOT awaited
  await window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({...})
  onSaved?.(newSettings)  // Callback fires even if voice settings fail
  onClose()
}
```

**Issue**: If `voiceApplySettings` fails, the modal closes and the error is silently swallowed. Voice settings changes won't persist.

---

**4. Inconsistent Empty Workspace Protection in Mobile Server (Lines 75-84)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts`

The POST handler (lines 74-84) checks `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` instead of `incoming?.projects?.length`. This causes it to:
- Never access the correct property
- Fall through to always being 0 when the path is wrong
- Save even invalid workspace payloads

```typescript
// Line 75 (WRONG)
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0

// Line 51 (CORRECT in PUT)
const incomingProjects = incoming?.projects?.length || 0
```

If a mobile client sends `{ workspace: { projects: [] } }` via POST, it passes validation (because `incoming?.workspace?.projects` evaluates to an empty array, not the expected `Workspace` object). Then `saveWorkspace({})` is called with an invalid structure.

---

**5. Missing Tmp File Cleanup on Save Failure (Lines 189-200)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

If `renameSync()` fails (line 197), the `.tmp` file is left behind:

```typescript
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written)
renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)  // If this fails, tmpPath is orphaned
```

On the next save, the old `.tmp` file might interfere with the write operation (especially on Windows where files can't be renamed while in use).

---

### HIGH PRIORITY ISSUES

**6. Settings Load Missing DefaultProjectDir Fallback (Line 237)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

```typescript
getSettings(): Settings {
  return this.data.settings ?? { 
    defaultProjectDir: '', 
    theme: 'default', 
    backend: 'default'  // Missing voiceOutputEnabled, backend defaults
  }
}
```

If `this.data.settings` is `undefined`, the returned object is incomplete. Consumer code expecting `voiceOutputEnabled` will get `undefined`, not a safe default.

---

**7. Workspace Loader Doesn't Handle Deleted Projects (useWorkspaceLoader.ts: Lines 102-109)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceLoader.ts`

When restoring tabs, the code looks up projects by path:
```typescript
const projectForTab = workspace.projects?.find((p: { path: string }) => p.path === savedTab.projectPath)
```

If that project was deleted from the workspace file, `projectForTab` is undefined, but there's no fallback to global settings. The tab will spawn with `undefined` backend.

---

**8. Meta-Projects Symlink Creation Doesn't Handle Collisions (Line 224)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/meta-project-sync.ts`

The `getUniqueSymlinkName()` function (lines 78-96) tries to generate unique names, but if two projects have the same name in the same category, it creates names like `ProjectName` and `ProjectName-2`. However, the desiredSymlinks map uses `projectPath` as the key (line 225), not the name. If the same path appears twice in the projects list, the second one overwrites the first in the map.

```typescript
const desiredSymlinks = new Map<string, string>()
for (const project of projects) {
  const symlinkName = getUniqueSymlinkName(categoryDir, project.name, usedNames)
  desiredSymlinks.set(project.path, symlinkName)  // Last one wins if duplicates
}
```

**Issue**: Duplicate projects (same path) will be silently deduplicated, potentially leaving orphaned symlinks.

---

**9. Backup Restoration Doesn't Update In-Memory State (Lines 142-151)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

When the main file is corrupted and backup is restored:
```typescript
const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
  console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup...')
  try {
    writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))  // Line 147
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
  }
  return backup  // In-memory state updated
}
```

The backup is written to disk (line 147), but if that write fails, the catch block (lines 148-150) logs the error but still returns the backup data. This causes in-memory state to diverge from disk state.

---

### MEDIUM PRIORITY ISSUES

**10. Workspace Save in MainApp Skips Save on Hot Reload (Lines 175-177)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx`

```typescript
if (projects.length === 0 && hadProjects) {
  console.warn('Skipping save: projects empty but previously had projects (likely hot reload)')
  return
}
```

This protection against hot-reload data loss is good, but it uses `sessionStorage` which can be cleared by the user or in some browsers. If sessionStorage is cleared, a legitimate empty project list will be saved.

---

**11. Settings Change Not Synchronized to All Components (SettingsModal.tsx: Line 178)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx`

The `onSaved` callback returns only general settings, but voice settings are saved separately. Components like `useSettings()` hook won't be notified of voice setting changes.

---

**12. Mobile Server POST /api/workspace Accepts Wrong Structure (Lines 67-89)**

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive audit. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive Audit Report: Workspace Persistence and Settings Code

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, I've identified several bugs and potential issues across the persistence layer. Here are my findings organized by severity:

### CRITICAL BUGS

**1. POST /api/workspace Endpoint: Incorrect Property Path (Line 75)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts`

The POST endpoint checks for `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` (line 75) while the PUT endpoint checks `incoming?.projects?.length` (line 51). This is inconsistent and will cause validation failures.

```
Line 75: const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
Line 78: const currentProjects = current?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
```

The SessionStore's `saveWorkspace()` method (session-store.ts:222) receives a `Workspace` object directly, not wrapped in a `{ workspace: ... }` structure. The POST endpoint is checking the wrong property path, causing its data validation to always fail silently.

**Expected behavior**: Both endpoints should check `incoming?.projects?.length`

---

**2. Race Condition in saveWorkspace() When Backup Update Fails (Lines 178-182)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

The backup is created AFTER validating the incoming workspace but BEFORE the atomic write completes. If the application crashes between creating the backup and completing the rename, the backup may be stale and recovery will restore outdated data.

```typescript
// Line 174-187: Backup logic
if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
  try {
    const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
    if (stat.size > 0) {
      const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
      if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
        writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
      }
    }
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)  // Silent failure
  }
}
// Line 189-197: Atomic write happens here
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
```

**Issue**: If backup update fails with a logged error, the save continues anyway to a temp file. But if the rename fails after that, the system falls back to the old corrupted main file (not the new backup).

---

**3. Settings Not Persisting in SettingsModal.tsx (Line 178)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx`

The `handleSave()` function saves application settings and voice settings separately, but the `onSaved` callback (line 178) passes only the `newSettings` object, omitting voice settings. The voice settings are saved via a separate call to `voiceApplySettings` (lines 169-177), but if that promise rejects, it's not awaited.

```typescript
async function handleSave(): Promise<void> {
  const newSettings = { ... }
  await window.electronAPI?.saveSettings(newSettings)
  // Line 169-177: This promise is NOT awaited
  await window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({...})
  onSaved?.(newSettings)  // Callback fires even if voice settings fail
  onClose()
}
```

**Issue**: If `voiceApplySettings` fails, the modal closes and the error is silently swallowed. Voice settings changes won't persist.

---

**4. Inconsistent Empty Workspace Protection in Mobile Server (Lines 75-84)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts`

The POST handler (lines 74-84) checks `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` instead of `incoming?.projects?.length`. This causes it to:
- Never access the correct property
- Fall through to always being 0 when the path is wrong
- Save even invalid workspace payloads

```typescript
// Line 75 (WRONG)
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0

// Line 51 (CORRECT in PUT)
const incomingProjects = incoming?.projects?.length || 0
```

If a mobile client sends `{ workspace: { projects: [] } }` via POST, it passes validation (because `incoming?.workspace?.projects` evaluates to an empty array, not the expected `Workspace` object). Then `saveWorkspace({})` is called with an invalid structure.

---

**5. Missing Tmp File Cleanup on Save Failure (Lines 189-200)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

If `renameSync()` fails (line 197), the `.tmp` file is left behind:

```typescript
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written)
renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)  // If this fails, tmpPath is orphaned
```

On the next save, the old `.tmp` file might interfere with the write operation (especially on Windows where files can't be renamed while in use).

---

### HIGH PRIORITY ISSUES

**6. Settings Load Missing DefaultProjectDir Fallback (Line 237)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

```typescript
getSettings(): Settings {
  return this.data.settings ?? { 
    defaultProjectDir: '', 
    theme: 'default', 
    backend: 'default'  // Missing voiceOutputEnabled, backend defaults
  }
}
```

If `this.data.settings` is `undefined`, the returned object is incomplete. Consumer code expecting `voiceOutputEnabled` will get `undefined`, not a safe default.

---

**7. Workspace Loader Doesn't Handle Deleted Projects (useWorkspaceLoader.ts: Lines 102-109)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/hooks/useWorkspaceLoader.ts`

When restoring tabs, the code looks up projects by path:
```typescript
const projectForTab = workspace.projects?.find((p: { path: string }) => p.path === savedTab.projectPath)
```

If that project was deleted from the workspace file, `projectForTab` is undefined, but there's no fallback to global settings. The tab will spawn with `undefined` backend.

---

**8. Meta-Projects Symlink Creation Doesn't Handle Collisions (Line 224)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/meta-project-sync.ts`

The `getUniqueSymlinkName()` function (lines 78-96) tries to generate unique names, but if two projects have the same name in the same category, it creates names like `ProjectName` and `ProjectName-2`. However, the desiredSymlinks map uses `projectPath` as the key (line 225), not the name. If the same path appears twice in the projects list, the second one overwrites the first in the map.

```typescript
const desiredSymlinks = new Map<string, string>()
for (const project of projects) {
  const symlinkName = getUniqueSymlinkName(categoryDir, project.name, usedNames)
  desiredSymlinks.set(project.path, symlinkName)  // Last one wins if duplicates
}
```

**Issue**: Duplicate projects (same path) will be silently deduplicated, potentially leaving orphaned symlinks.

---

**9. Backup Restoration Doesn't Update In-Memory State (Lines 142-151)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`

When the main file is corrupted and backup is restored:
```typescript
const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
  console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup...')
  try {
    writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))  // Line 147
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
  }
  return backup  // In-memory state updated
}
```

The backup is written to disk (line 147), but if that write fails, the catch block (lines 148-150) logs the error but still returns the backup data. This causes in-memory state to diverge from disk state.

---

### MEDIUM PRIORITY ISSUES

**10. Workspace Save in MainApp Skips Save on Hot Reload (Lines 175-177)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/App/MainApp.tsx`

```typescript
if (projects.length === 0 && hadProjects) {
  console.warn('Skipping save: projects empty but previously had projects (likely hot reload)')
  return
}
```

This protection against hot-reload data loss is good, but it uses `sessionStorage` which can be cleared by the user or in some browsers. If sessionStorage is cleared, a legitimate empty project list will be saved.

---

**11. Settings Change Not Synchronized to All Components (SettingsModal.tsx: Line 178)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/SettingsModal.tsx`

The `onSaved` callback returns only general settings, but voice settings are saved separately. Components like `useSettings()` hook won't be notified of voice setting changes.

---

**12. Mobile Server POST /api/workspace Accepts Wrong Structure (Lines 67-89)**
**File**: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts`

The endpoint calls `saveWorkspace(req.body)` directly (line 84), but the validation checks `req.body?.workspace?.projects`. If the mobile client sends the correct structure `{ projects: [...], openTabs: [...] }`, the validation fails because it looks for nested `workspace` property.

---

### RECOMMENDATIONS

1. **Fix POST workspace validation** to use `incoming?.projects?.length`
2. **Add tmp file cleanup** on save failure with a try-finally block
3. **Await voiceApplySettings** in SettingsModal and handle rejection
4. **Add comprehensive defaults** in getSettings() for all expected fields
5. **Add project existence validation** in workspace loader
6. **Add synchronization queue** to prevent concurrent save operations
7. **Improve error recovery** to distinguish between corrupted and missing files
8. **Document expected structure** for mobile/HTTP clients vs Electron clients
9. **Add unit tests** for backup/restore scenarios and concurrent access
10. **Validate workspace structure** before saving (not just project count)

---

## Summary of Files Audited

1. `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts` - Core persistence
2. `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/meta-project-sync.ts` - Symlink management
3. `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts` - HTTP endpoints
